title: torilis nodosa subsp. webbii
intro: Torilis nodosa subsp. webbii, also known as Webb's hedge parsley, is a subspecies of the Torilis nodosa plant, which belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, with medicinal properties attributed to its essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Torilis nodosa subsp. webbii is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits and take advantage of its medicinal properties.
